description.en: To verify the Superposition Theorem by recording the branch currents under three conditions: (i) with only the current source active, (ii) with only the voltage source active, and (iii) with both sources connected
Click INSTRUCTION to open the detailed procedure for performing all three cases of the Superposition Theorem experiment.
To use this feature, click on the “AI Guide” button. The AI guide will assist you in performing the simulation accurately at each step.
Press CHECK after wiring each case. Sources should be switched ON only after the current case connections are verified.
Click Calculate after completing all observations. It auto-fills resistance values, source values and observed currents in the Calculation Panel.

Click on the AUTO CONNECT button to automatically make the required connections for the selected case as per the given instructions.
Case 1: Connects the Current Source only and short-circuits the voltage source.
Case 2: Connects the Voltage Source only and open-circuits the current source.
Case 3: Connects both the Current Source and Voltage Source.
Click ADD after each verified case. The observation table stores one row each for current source only, voltage source only, and both sources connected.
Click RESET to clear the circuit connections, source values, resistance values, observations, and calculation entries.
Click PRINT to print the current experiment page after completing the observations.
Set R1, R2, and R3 before making connections. These resistance values must remain the same for all three cases in one experiment run.

The current source supplies a fixed current to the circuit. During the Current Source Only case, activate this source and set the desired current value. The resulting branch currents I1, I2, and I3 will be used later for Superposition calculations.
A1, A2 and A3 show the branch current readings. Note these values for Current Source Only, Voltage Source Only and Both Sources Active cases.

The voltage source supplies a fixed voltage to the circuit. During the Voltage Source Only case, activate this source and set the desired voltage value. The resulting branch currents I1, I2, and I3 will be used later for Superposition calculations.
Make and verify the circuit connections separately for each case: current source only, voltage source only, and both sources connected.
This table displays the measured branch currents I1, I2, and I3 for all three cases. These readings are used later in the calculation panel.
After completing all three observations, enter the resistance values, source values, current source readings, and voltage source readings in this section.
After completing the observation table and calculation verification, generate the final report containing readings, calculations, verification status, and conclusion.
